description_long: This is a military-style twin-engine propeller airplane with a high-wing configuration and a robust, utilitarian design. The fuselage is painted primarily in a light gray or off-white color, accented with blue stripes and military insignia, including a star roundel and identification markings on the tail. The wings are straight and mounted high on the fuselage, with two propeller engines positioned on each wing. The tail section features a conventional vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizer, with the tailplane mounted low. The cockpit is located at the front of the fuselage, with multiple windows providing visibility for the crew. The landing gear appears to be fixed and tricycle in configuration, suitable for rugged or unprepared surfaces.
description: A light gray, twin-propeller military airplane with high-mounted straight wings, blue accents, and a conventional tail. Features a forward cockpit and fixed tricycle landing gear.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full fuselage, high-mounted wings, propellers, and detailed tail markings.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the straight wingspan, twin engines, and the tail's horizontal and vertical stabilizers.
description_view_2: Opposite side view mirroring view 0, with clear visibility of the cockpit, engines, and military insignia.
description_view_3: Top-down view displaying the full wingspan, engine placement, and the overall planform of the airplane.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: High-wing, straight wings with no visible winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Twin turboprop engines mounted on the wings.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and low-mounted horizontal stabilizer.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed tricycle landing gear with two main gear units and a single nose gear.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with multiple windows, likely multi-seat.
